prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with models tracking trends differently (ONLY_CWT_catboost best at 0.73 slope err vs NaiveSeasonal poor at 4.24). Seasonality capture varies dramatically: NaiveSeasonal leads at 0.90 correlation while svr-family models lag below 0.35. Different horizons favor different models (rf/ETS early, ONLY_DWT_catboost late), confirming that equal-weighting is suboptimal. DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and horizon dimensions.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "rf", "late": "ONLY_DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_svr", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9718,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with models tracking trends differently (ONLY_CWT_catboost best at 0.73 slope err vs NaiveSeasonal poor at 4.24). Seasonality capture varies dramatically: NaiveSeasonal leads at 0.90 correlation while svr-family models lag below 0.35. Different horizons favor different models (rf/ETS early, ONLY_DWT_catboost late), confirming that equal-weighting is suboptimal. DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and horizon dimensions."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage issues - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no candidate beating all current set members. Score margin between top2 is small (0.0016) indicating competitive performance. No redundancy concerns - 2 selection candidates use different methods (best-per-horizon vs top-k mean).",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with models tracking trends differently (ONLY_CWT_catboost best at 0.73 slope err vs NaiveSeasonal poor at 4.24). Seasonality capture varies dramatically: NaiveSeasonal leads at 0.90 correlation while svr-family models lag below 0.35. Different horizons favor different models (rf/ETS early, ONLY_DWT_catboost late), confirming that equal-weighting is suboptimal. DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across trend, seasonality, and horizon dimensions.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.2719 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.9718 confirm extreme model disagreement favoring dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. RMSE gap between ETS(2380.87) and svr(4469.77) is ~88%, justifying inverse_rmse_weights for model dispersion handling.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage issues - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no candidate beating all current set members. Score margin between top2 is small (0.0016) indicating competitive performance. No redundancy concerns - 2 selection candidates use different methods (best-per-horizon vs top-k mean).
